Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff To Lift The Stay
Off Calendar Mar-29-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael DiSanto,Esq., and Asim M. bhansali, esq., of Kwun Bhansali Lazarus, 415-630-2352 appearing for Plaintiff;
David McMonigle, Esq., Long & Levit LLP, 415-397-2222, appearing for DefendantCotchett, Pitre & McCarthy et al;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MICHAEL DISANTO'S Motion To Lift The Stay.

Plaintiff Michael DiSanto's motion to lift stay is denied. The arbitration provision in plaintiff's retainer agreement with defendant law firm states that "any dispute" relating to that agreement "shall be submitted to JAMS in San Francisco, CA, or if that forum is not available, to another equivalent alternative dispute resolution forum." A "forum" is "a court or arbitral tribunal - each with its own set of rules and procedures," and each ADR provider has its own set of rules and procedures. (Alan v. Sup. Ct. (2003) 111 Cal.App.4th 217, 224.) JAMS having disqualified itself, the dispute "shall be submitted" to an alternative ADR provider.

Plaintiff's cited cases do not help him. Unlike the agreement in Martinez v. Master Protection Corporation (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 107, the arbitration agreement here does not mandate one exclusive arbitral forum, but instead provides for an "equivalent" ADR alternative if JAMS is unavailable. Moreover, contracts are not interpreted against their drafters when, as here, they are unambiguous. (Badie v. Bank of America (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 779, 801.)

The parties are ordered to meet and confer to select an ADR provider for their arbitration.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Unopposed Motion To Seal
Off Calendar Aug-20-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kate E. Lazarus, Esq. of Kwun Bhansali Lazarus, 415.630.2353, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Joseph P. McMonigle, Esq. of Long & Levit LLP, 415.438.4534, appearing for the Defendants.

No argument held on this matter.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Philip Gregory and Cotchett, Pitre & McCarthy, LLP's motion to seal redacted portions of Mr. Gregory's declaration and defendants' memorandum filed July 20, 2018 is granted. No opposition filed and defendants have established the requirements for their requested sealing order per CRC 2.550. Order signed. (see order for full details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com/mtorreano@sftc.org. =(302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants Cotchett Pitre And Mccarthy Llp And Philip Gregory To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Aug-20-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kate E. Lazarus, Esq. of Kwun Bhansali Lazarus, 415.630.2353, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Joseph P. McMonigle, Esq. of Long & Levit LLP, 415.438.4534, appearing for the Defendants.

Ruling: ARGUED. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Philip Gregory and Cotchett, Pitre & McCarthy, LLP's motion to compel arbitration and stay proceedings of all claims in the complaint filed by plaintiff Michael DiSanto is granted. This case is stayed pending conclusion of arbitration proceedings between the parties. Absent a separate written agreement governing CPM's representation of Mr. DiSanto for Bingham's counterclaims against Mr. DiSanto, the arbitration agreement in the Retainer Agreement covers CPM's representation of Mr. DiSanto as to the counterclaims and requires that the parties arbitrate all claims alleged by Mr. DiSanto in this case. The evidence on this motion shows that prior to signing the Retainer Agreement, the parties contemplated the possibility that Bingham might assert counterclaims against Mr. DiSanto relating to Mr. DiSanto's employment agreement with Bingham. While CPM and Mr. DiSanto did not enter into a separate written agreement explicitly providing for CPM to defend Mr. DiSanto against Bingham's counterclaims, CPM represented Mr. DiSanto for the entire action and advanced the costs of arbitration for all claims and counterclaims per the Retainer Agreement. Mr. DiSanto thereafter refused to reimburse CPM for the advanced costs, arguing that per the Retainer Agreement, CPM was not entitled to reimbursement since Mr. DiSanto did not recover anything in the arbitration. Order signed. (see order for full details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com/mtorreano@sftc.org. =(302/HEK)
